It is the highest and decisions reached at its previous meetings. Furthermore, representative body of the Group and has an expert committee regarding each committee, copies of the minutes of these on Sustainability. The policy of the Board is to delegate the decisions, as well as the documentation corresponding to the ordinary management of the Company and the execution of its matters covered in them, are made available to the members of strategy to the executive bodies and the management team and the Board through the specific tool set up for this purpose. to concentrate its activity on the general supervisory function, assuming and exercising directly and in a non-delegable way the responsibilities that this function entails, as provided for in the Law, the Corporate By-Laws and the Regulations governing the Board of Directors. In terms of sustainability, there are two committees that are particularly involved with matters of sustainability and climate change:

Impact of ESG factors on real estate risk Real estate risk can be affected by climate factors, both physical (with a minor impact since it would affect a small number of properties) and transition (influencing the market price, and devaluation would increase real estate risk) so it could reach a level of materiality from low in the short term to high in the long term, mainly due to the risks of transition. Impact of ESG factors on real estate risk Real estate risk can be affected by climate factors, both physical (with a minor impact since it would affect a small number of properties) and transition (influencing the market price, and devaluation would increase real estate risk) so it could reach a level of materiality from low in the short term to high in the long term, mainly due to the risks of transition. Impact of ESG Factors on Sovereign Risk Sovereign risk may be affected by the transition risks that are expected to have a low impact because Italy, the country where foreign sovereign debt is concentrated, will comply with directives imposed by the European Union, as will the other member countries, although it will increase to a medium impact as the target date of 2050 approaches.

Impact of ESG factors on market risk Market risk can be affected by both climate factors, linked to physical and transition risks. These risks would not have a high impact. The greatest impact derives from transition risks, especially related to policies, and changes in consumer and investor preferences, reaching a High level of impact in the long term. Impact of ESG factors on market risk Market risk can be affected by both climate factors, linked to physical and transition risks. These risks would not have a high impact. The greatest impact derives from transition risks, especially related to policies, and changes in consumer and investor preferences, reaching a High level of impact in the long term. Impact of ESG factors on liquidity and financing risk Liquidity and financing risk may be affected by climate factors linked to both physical and transition risks, due to the need for affected customers to withdraw deposits to gain liquidity in order to cope with losses or refinance their activities. In this case, given the diversity of the portfolio itself, it would not have a significant impact for the Group. With regard to transition risks, this could become significant in the long term, in particular as regards consumer and investor preferences.
